subject: science
question_text: The passage below describes an experiment. an icy pavement After a severe winter storm, Wendell's driveway was covered with ice. He read that salt makes ice melt at a lower temperature. Before covering his entire driveway with salt, he wanted to know if adding salt could actually help melt ice in the freezing outdoor temperatures. Wendell weighed twenty ice cubes. He sprinkled salt on half of the ice cubes and left the other half unsalted. He placed all the ice cubes outside. One hour later, Wendell quickly dried each ice cube and reweighed it to see how much it had melted. In this experiment, which were part of an experimental group?

subject: science
question_text: The passage below describes an experiment. Read the passage and think about the variables that are described. a hospital bed with plastic bed rails Many types of bacteria cannot survive on objects made of copper. Dr Li was considering using beds with copper bed rails instead of beds with plastic rails at the hospital where she worked. She wanted to know if copper bed rails would reduce the number of patients who got new infections. To test this, Dr Li had beds with copper rails placed in half of the hospital rooms. Over the next six months, 430 patients were admitted to the hospital: 215 patients were put in rooms with copper bed rails and 215 patients were put in rooms with plastic bed rails. Dr Li counted the number of patients in each type of room who got new infections while they were in the hospital. Which of the following was a dependent variable in this experiment?
options: the number of patients who got new infections
correct_answer: the number of patients who got new infections

subject: science
question_text: The passage below describes an experiment. Read the passage and then follow the instructions below. a dome of water on the surface of a penny Lewis used a dropper to put equal-sized drops of pure water, one at a time, onto a penny. The drops stayed together and formed a dome on the penny's surface. Lewis recorded the number of drops he could add before the water spilled over the edge of the penny. Then, he rinsed and dried the penny, and repeated the test using water mixed with hand soap. He repeated these trials on nine additional pennies. Lewis compared the average number of pure water drops to the average number of water drops mixed with hand soap that he could add to a penny before the water spilled over. Identify the question that Lewis's experiment can best answer.
options: Can pennies hold more drops of water mixed with washing-up liquid or water mixed with hand soap?
Can pennies hold more drops of pure water or water mixed with hand soap?
correct_answer: Can pennies hold more drops of pure water or water mixed with hand soap?

subject: science
skill_name: Identify questions that can be investigated with a set of materials
question_text: Riley is washing dishes after cooking his dinner. He notices that some of the dishes still feel oily after being washed. He wonders what factors affect how clean dishes are after they are washed. So, she decides to design an experiment. She has the following supplies available: a sponge liquid dish detergent a bar of soap hot water cold water a pile of dirty dishes Using only these supplies, which question can Riley investigate with an experiment?
options: Are dishes cleaner when washed with liquid dish detergent or with bar soap?
Are dishes washed in a dishwasher cleaner than dishes washed by hand?
Are dishes cleaner when washed with a soapy sponge or with a soapy rag?
correct_answer: Are dishes cleaner when washed with a soapy sponge or with a soapy rag?

subject: science
question_text: People can use the engineering-design process to develop solutions to problems. One step in the process is testing if a potential solution meets the requirements of the design. The passage below describes how the engineering-design process was used to test a solution to a problem. Read the passage. Then answer the question below. an engineer at a water treatment plant Samuel was an engineer at a water treatment plant. At the plant, an expensive filter was used to remove disease-causing bacteria from the water. But over time, the filter would become clogged with bacteria. If the filter became clogged, the water would not move through quickly enough. Samuel had to decide when the filter was too clogged and needed to be replaced. So, during his inspection, Samuel checked the filter by measuring how quickly water moved through it. Which of the following could Samuel's test show?
options: whether an inexpensive filter would become clogged more often
the amount of bacteria in the water before it was filtered

subject: science
skill_name: What are atoms and chemical elements?
question_text: Though they are too small to see without a special microscope, atoms make up all of the substances around you. Like all matter, atoms have mass and volume. But atoms are extremely small. A water droplet, like the one hanging from the tip of the leaf in this picture, contains more than a billion trillion atoms! Which of the following statements is true?
options: Atoms can be seen with the naked eye.
Atoms make up every substance around you.

question_text: The model below represents potassium bromide. In some countries, potassium bromide can be used as medicine to treat seizures. K Br Complete the statement. Potassium bromide is

subject: science
question_text: This passage describes a chemical reaction. Read the passage. Then, follow the instructions below. Did you know that lightning helps provide nutrients to plants on Earth? When lightning strikes, the air around it becomes extremely hot. This leads to the formation of nitric oxide from the nitrogen and oxygen gases in the air. Nitric oxide plays an important role in helping plants grow. Complete the sentence. In this chemical reaction, nitric oxide is a ﻿.

subject: science
skill_name: Describe energy changes in chemical reactions
question_text: When a chemical reaction absorbs or releases thermal energy, the reaction causes a change in temperature. Read the passage about a chemical reaction that absorbs or releases thermal energy. Then, follow the instructions below. Airbags reduce injuries during car crashes by inflating quickly to provide cushions for people inside a car. Older airbags contain a small amount of sodium azide (NaN 3 ) . If a crash occurs, the sodium azide is heated, causing it to break down into sodium metal (Na) and nitrogen gas (N 2 ) . In less than one-tenth of a second, enough nitrogen gas is produced to completely fill the airbag. As a result of the reaction, thermal energy is transferred into the surroundings. Complete the statement. During this chemical reaction, the airbag becomes ﻿.

subject: science
question_text: A sailboat is speeding up as a gust of wind catches the sail. Which statement describes the sailboat's motion?
options: The sailboat has a constant velocity.

subject: science
skill_name: Predict forces using Newton’s third law of motion
question_text: Isaac Newton was born in the 1600s and studied how objects move. He discovered three fundamental laws about forces and motion. According to Newton's third law, for every force, there is an equal and opposite force. Consider the following force: Earth is pulling on the Moon. According to Newton's third law, what other force must be happening?
options: The Moon is pushing on Earth.
The Moon is pulling on Earth.
correct_answer: The Moon is pulling on Earth.

subject: science
question_text: Mia is walking her dog, Bamboo. A squirrel just ran by, and Bamboo is pulling forward on the leash with a force of 250N. Mia is pulling backward on the leash with a force of 180N. Assume all other forces on the leash are balanced. Which statement describes the forces on the leash?
options: The forces are balanced, so there is no net force on the leash.
The forces are unbalanced, so there is a net force on the leash.
correct_answer: The forces are unbalanced, so there is a net force on the leash.

subject: science
skill_name: Identify changes in gravitational potential energy
question_text: Read the text about an object in motion. On a snowy day, Min rode her sled down a big hill. Complete the statement. Assume that the sled's mass did not change. The gravitational potential energy stored between the sled and Earth ﻿ as Min rode down the hill.

subject: science
skill_name: Explore energy transformations: roller coaster ride
question_text: There are many types of energy. One type is kinetic energy, which is the energy an object has when it is moving. Read the passage about the start of a rollercoaster ride and think about the kinetic energy of the rollercoaster train. At a funfair, a group of riders boarded a rollercoaster train and sat down. Once everyone was safely in their seats, the ride operator flipped a switch, and electricity flowed to a motor below the track. The motor pulled the train forward and out of the station. Which of the following is better evidence that the train's kinetic energy changed?
options: The train was stopped, and then it began to move forward and away from the station.
Electricity started flowing to the motor when the ride operator flipped the switch.
correct_answer: Electricity started flowing to the motor when the ride operator flipped the switch.

subject: science
question_text: There are many types of energy. One type is kinetic energy, which is the energy an object has when it is moving. Read the passage about a bike ride. Think about the kinetic energy of the person riding the bike. Ellen was training for a bike race. One afternoon, she grabbed her helmet and took her bike out to the street. She got on the bike and started to pedal, pushing hard to start moving forward. Which of the following is better evidence that Ellen's kinetic energy changed?
options: When Ellen started pedalling her bike, she began moving along the street.
When Ellen got on her bike, her feet were no longer touching the ground.
correct_answer: When Ellen got on her bike, her feet were no longer touching the ground.

subject: science
question_text: The images below show two pairs of magnets. The magnets in different pairs do not affect each other. All the magnets shown are made of the same material, but some of them are different sizes. Pair 1 N S N S 3.15 cm Pair 2 S N N S 3.15 cm Think about the magnetic force between the magnets in each pair. Which of the following statements is true?
options: The magnitude of the magnetic force is greater in Pair 1.
The magnitude of the magnetic force is greater in Pair 2.
The magnitude of the magnetic force is the same in both pairs.
correct_answer: The magnitude of the magnetic force is the same in both pairs.

skill_name: Predict heat flow and temperature changes
question_text: Two solid blocks are heated to the temperatures shown. The blocks are placed so they touch. Which diagram shows the direction heat will flow?

subject: science
question_text: Two bottles of soda were sitting on a porch on a hot day. The two bottles were identical, except that one bottle was made of brown glass and one bottle was made of clear glass. This table shows how the temperature of each bottle changed over 25minutes. Bottle Initial temperature (°C) Final temperature (°C) Clear bottle 18 23 Brown bottle 18 28 During this time, thermal energy was transferred from ﻿ to ﻿.

subject: science
skill_name: Identify how particle motion affects temperature and pressure
question_text: The diagrams below show two pure samples of gas in identical closed, rigid containers. Each coloured ball represents one gas particle. Both samples have the same number of particles. Sample A Mass of each particle: 28 u Average particle speed: 1100 m/s Sample B Mass of each particle: 44 u Average particle speed: 1300 m/s Compare the average kinetic energies of the particles in each sample. Which sample has the higher temperature?
options: neither; the samples have the same temperature
correct_answer: neither; the samples have the same temperature

question_text: The diagram below is a model of two solutions. Each green ball represents one particle of solute. Solvent volume: 30 mL Solvent volume: 30 mL
options: neither; their concentrations are the same
correct_answer: neither; their concentrations are the same

subject: science
question_text: The diagram below shows a solution with one solute. Each solute particle is represented by a yellow ball. The solution fills a closed container that is divided in half by a membrane. The membrane, represented by a dotted line, is permeable to the solute particles. The diagram shows how the solution can change over time during the process of diffusion. Complete the text to describe the diagram. Solute particles moved in both directions across the permeable membrane. But more solute particles moved across the membrane ﻿. When there was an equal concentration on both sides, the particles reached equilibrium.
options: from the right to the left
from the left to the right
correct_answer: from the left to the right

It was named after the painter Georgia O'Keeffe because she lived nearby. What is this archosaur's scientific name?

subject: science
question_text: Select the one true statement.
options: In plant cells, the vacuoles contain the master plan for cell activities and cell development.
The Golgi is outside the nucleus in a plant cell.
An animal cell can have a Golgi but does not have endoplasmic reticulum.
correct_answer: An animal cell can have a Golgi but does not have endoplasmic reticulum.

question_text: The diagram shows phases of the cell cycle in animal cells. During which phase does the cytoplasm divide and the two daughter cells separate?

question_text: Complete the sentence. Animals produce waste, which ﻿.
options: is used to help the animal grow
all stays in the animal's body forever
correct_answer: all stays in the animal's body forever

question_text: This diagram shows fossils in an undisturbed sedimentary rock sequence. Which of the following fossils is older? Select the more likely answer.

question_text: This diagram shows the life cycle of an apple tree. Sperm cells are found inside pollen. What kind of cells are sperm?

subject: science
question_text: This diagram shows the life cycle of a fern. Which plant can produce eggs and sperm?

subject: science
question_text: Use the evidence in the text to select the photosynthetic organism.
options: Velvet ants look like ants covered in red hairs, but these insects are actually wasps! Velvet ants get sugars by eating nectar. The velvet ants use these sugars to get energy.
Kangaroo paws are Australian plants that have fine red hairs covering their flowers and leaves. Kangaroo paw plants combine carbon dioxide and water to make sugars that the plants use as food.
correct_answer: Kangaroo paws are Australian plants that have fine red hairs covering their flowers and leaves. Kangaroo paw plants combine carbon dioxide and water to make sugars that the plants use as food.

subject: science
question_text: Read the passage. Then answer the question below. American lotus plants in a lake In a small lake in Wisconsin, aquatic plants such as water milfoil, American lotus, and water purslane grow along the shoreline. These plants help to keep the lake clean by absorbing substances that can pollute the water. The plants also provide oxygen to aquatic animals and create a habitat for fish, frogs, and insects. Many insects and small fish hide in these aquatic plants. So, the plants are a common hunting area for predatory species such as bluegill fish. Which of the following best describes a community in a small lake in Wisconsin?
options: the insects, the water milfoil, and the nutrients
the water milfoil, the American lotus, and the water purslane
correct_answer: the water milfoil, the American lotus, and the water purslane

subject: science
question_text: Select the tropical rain forest ecosystem.
options: This ecosystem has: warm summers and warm winters a rainy season and a dry season soil that is poor in nutrients
This ecosystem has: warm, wet summers and cold, wet winters soil that is rich in nutrients only a few types of trees
This ecosystem has: year-round rain and warm temperatures soil that is poor in nutrients many different types of organisms
correct_answer: This ecosystem has: year-round rain and warm temperatures soil that is poor in nutrients many different types of organisms

subject: science
question_text: Read the passage. Then answer the question. a flea holding onto a dog's hair Fleas are small insects that feed on the blood of mammals, including dogs. They use a specialised mouth to pierce a mammal's skin and suck its blood. A flea can drink up to 15 times its body weight in blood each day! When a flea feeds on a dog's blood, the flea's saliva can irritate the dog's skin. Also, the flea may transmit, or pass, diseases to the dog. Which type of relationship is formed when a flea feeds on a dog's blood?

subject: science
skill_name: Coral reef biodiversity and human uses: evaluate solutions
question_text: Imagine a small island named Seafoam Island that is facing the following problem: Seafoam Island is surrounded by a tropical coral reef called Seafoam Reef. Many tourists come to see Seafoam Reef, and Seafoam Island's residents earn money from these visitors. But as tourism has increased, more boats have started anchoring over the reef. The boats' anchors have hit and damaged corals. Seaweed often grows over damaged corals. Scientists on Seafoam Island have projected that if nothing is done to stop anchor damage, most of Seafoam Reef will eventually be covered with seaweed. If this happens, the reef will lose biodiversity, which means it will have fewer species. The residents of Seafoam Island will also lose important ecosystem services, such as money from tourism. a scientist studying a reef covered in seaweed The Seafoam Island Government Council has the job of finding a solution to the coral damage problem. However, finding the best solution is not easy. Seafoam Island has several major interest groups, or groups of people with important and distinct concerns about the problem. Each group has different requirements for a solution. One major interest group is concerned about the biodiversity of the reef. This group wants a solution that protects many different species. Another major interest group is concerned about jobs for Seafoam Island residents. This group wants a solution that keeps residents from losing money from tourism. The council wants to find a solution that will satisfy as many of the major interest groups on Seafoam Island as possible. What should the council do first?
options: The council should figure out which solution will save Seafoam Island the most money.
The council should find a solution that will protect as many species on Seafoam Reef as possible.
The council should determine each major interest group's requirements for the solution.
correct_answer: The council should determine each major interest group's requirements for the solution.

subject: science
skill_name: Evaluate claims about natural resource use: fossil fuels
question_text: Every day, humans all around the world use energy. They use energy to drive vehicles, heat and cool buildings, operate appliances and manufacture products. The graph below shows global energy use per year from 1965 to 2015. Energy use is measured using the unit terawatt-hours (TWh). Data sources: Our World in Data and BP Statistical Review of World Energy Select the statement that is supported by the data.
options: Humans used about the same amount of energy each year between 1965 and 2015.
Global energy use increased most years between 1965 and 2015.
correct_answer: Global energy use increased most years between 1965 and 2015.

subject: science
skill_name: Classify rocks as igneous, sedimentary or metamorphic
question_text: This is a piece of andesite. It was collected near a volcano in Greece. Andesite is commonly found near volcanoes that are close to oceans. It forms through the cooling of melted rock. The mineral grains in andesite are usually small in size. What type of rock is andesite?

subject: science
skill_name: Describe tectonic plate boundaries around the world
question_text: Read the passage and look at the picture. The Queen Charlotte Fault marks a boundary between the Pacific Plate and the North American Plate. The two plates slide past each other along this fault, which lies off the west coast of British Columbia. A magnitude 8.1 earthquake occurred along this fault in 1949, forming a crack in Earth's crust over 500 kilometres long. Complete the sentence. The Queen Charlotte Fault formed at a boundary.

subject: science
question_text: The map below shows humidity in the lower atmosphere on February 21, 2017. The map shows specific humidity, a measurement of the amount of water vapour in the air. The outlined area shows an air mass that influenced weather in Africa on that day. Look at the map. Then, answer the question below. Data source: United States National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration/Earth System Research Laboratory, Physical Sciences Division Which specific humidity level was measured within the outlined area shown?
options: 2 grams of water vapour per kilogram of air
11 grams of water vapour per kilogram of air
13 grams of water vapour per kilogram of air
correct_answer: 13 grams of water vapour per kilogram of air

subject: science
question_text: Air masses influence weather everywhere on Earth. But air masses can form only over certain places. These places are known as air mass source regions. Source regions are places that have low wind speeds for days or weeks at a time. When wind speeds are low, air above the source region remains in place. When air remains in place, it can take on the features of the source region. For example, air that remains over a warm source region can increase in temperature as the air gains thermal energy from Earth's surface. The map below shows wind speeds in Earth's lower atmosphere on 1 March 2017. Two areas, labelled A and B, are outlined on the map. Data source: United States National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration/Earth System Research Laboratory, Physical Sciences Division Based on the wind speeds measured this day, which area was more likely to be an air mass source region?

subject: science
question_text: São Paulo, Brazil, is a city in South America. It has a humid subtropical climate. Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec 5 10 15 20 25 30 35 40 Jan 0 Temperature (°C) Average temperature in São Paulo, Brazil Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec 0 20 40 60 80 100 120 140 160 180 200 220 240 Precipitation (mm) Average precipitation in São Paulo, Brazil Which statement best describes the climate of São Paulo? Hint: Summers in the Southern Hemisphere occur in December, January, and February. Winters in the Southern Hemisphere occur in June, July, and August.
options: Winters have slightly lower temperatures and more precipitation than summers.
Summers have higher temperatures and more precipitation than winters on average.
On average, the summer months are drier but have about the same temperature as the winter months.
correct_answer: On average, the summer months are drier but have about the same temperature as the winter months.

subject: science
question_text: Waikoloa Village and Mauna Kea are two locations on the island of Hawaii in the United States. Waikoloa Village is a small town near the island's west coast. Mauna Kea is Hawaii's highest mountain. The graph shows average monthly temperatures for Waikoloa Village and the summit of Mauna Kea. Which location has warmer average temperatures? Waikoloa Village Mauna Kea Submit

subject: science
skill_name: Analyse models of the Earth-Sun-Moon system
question_text: Our solar system is made up of the Sun and all the objects that move around it. These objects include planets, moons, asteroids and comets. The sizes of the objects in the solar system are difficult to imagine without the help of a model. Models make certain characteristics of a system easier to understand. A model can be a physical object, a graph, a diagram or a simulation. The diagram below is a model that shows the relative sizes of the Sun, the Moon and Earth. The two small dots represent the accurate sizes of Earth and the Moon compared to the Sun. A close-up view of Earth and the Moon is also shown. Complete the sentence to estimate the diameter of the Sun compared to Earth. The Sun's diameter is about ﻿ times greater than Earth's.
